note: SPECTRA reference — all 18 CIS Controls with key safeguards and Implementation Group assignments

controls.name: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
controls.description: Actively manage (inventory, track, and correct) all enterprise assets (end-user devices, including portable and mobile; network devices; non-computing/IoT devices; and servers) connected to the infrastructure, physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments, to accurately know the totality of assets that need to be monitored and protected within the enterprise.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain Detailed Enterprise Asset Inventory
Address Unauthorized Assets
Utilize an Active Discovery Tool
Use Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) Logging to Update Enterprise Asset Inventory

controls.name: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
controls.description: Actively manage (inventory, track, and correct) all software (operating systems and applications) on the network so that only authorized software is installed and can execute, and that unauthorized and unmanaged software is found and prevented from installation or execution.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain a Software Inventory
Ensure Authorized Software is Currently Supported
Address Unauthorized Software
Allowlist Authorized Software

controls.name: Data Protection
controls.description: Develop processes and technical controls to identify, classify, securely handle, retain, and dispose of data. This includes data stored on-site, at a remote location, or in cloud environments.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain a Data Management Process
Configure Data Access Control Lists
Enforce Data Retention
Encrypt Sensitive Data at Rest

controls.name: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.description: Establish and maintain the secure configuration of enterprise assets (end-user devices, including portable and mobile; network devices; non-computing/IoT devices; and servers) and software (operating systems and applications).

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process
Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process for Network Infrastructure
Manage Default Accounts on Enterprise Assets and Software
Uninstall or Disable Unnecessary Services on Enterprise Assets and Software

controls.name: Account Management
controls.description: Use processes and tools to assign and manage authorization to credentials for user accounts, including administrator accounts, as well as service accounts, to enterprise assets and software.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
Use Unique Passwords
Disable Dormant Accounts
Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts

controls.name: Access Control Management
controls.description: Use processes and tools to create, assign, manage, and revoke access credentials and privileges for user, administrator, and service accounts for enterprise assets and software.

safeguards.name: Establish an Access Granting Process
Establish an Access Revoking Process
Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
Require MFA for Administrative Access

controls.name: Continuous Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Develop a plan to continuously assess and track vulnerabilities on all enterprise assets within the enterprise's infrastructure, in order to remediate, and minimize, the window of opportunity for attackers.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain a Vulnerability Management Process
Establish and Maintain a Remediation Process
Perform Automated Operating System Patch Management
Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Internal Enterprise Assets

controls.name: Audit Log Management
controls.description: Collect, alert, review, and retain audit logs of events that could help detect, understand, or recover from an attack.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain an Audit Log Management Process
Collect Audit Logs
Collect Detailed Audit Logs
Centralize Audit Logs

controls.name: Email and Web Browser Protections
controls.description: Improve protections and detections of threats from email and web vectors, as these are opportunities for attackers to manipulate human behavior through direct engagement.

safeguards.name: Ensure Use of Only Fully Supported Browsers and Email Clients
Use DNS Filtering Services
Maintain and Enforce Network-Based URL Filters
Block Unnecessary File Types

controls.name: Malware Defenses
controls.description: Prevent or control the installation, spread, and execution of malicious applications, code, or scripts on enterprise assets.

safeguards.name: Deploy and Maintain Anti-Malware Software
Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Signature Updates
Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Scanning of Removable Media
Use Behavior-Based Anti-Malware Software

controls.name: Data Recovery
controls.description: Establish and maintain data recovery practices sufficient to restore in-scope enterprise assets to a pre-incident and trusted state.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
Perform Automated Backups
Protect Recovery Data
Establish and Maintain an Isolated Instance of Recovery Data

controls.name: Network Infrastructure Management
controls.description: Establish and maintain the secure configuration and management of network infrastructure devices (physical and virtual), including firewalls, routers, and switches.

safeguards.name: Ensure Network Infrastructure is Up-to-Date
Establish and Maintain a Secure Network Architecture
Securely Manage Network Infrastructure
Use of Secure Network Management and Communication Protocols

controls.name: Network Monitoring and Defense
controls.description: Operate processes and tooling to establish and maintain comprehensive network monitoring and defense against security threats across the enterprise's network infrastructure and user base.

safeguards.name: Centralize Security Event Alerting
Deploy a Network Intrusion Detection Solution
Collect Network Traffic Flow Logs
Deploy a Network Intrusion Prevention Solution

controls.name: Security Awareness and Skills Training
controls.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness program to influence behavior among the workforce to be security conscious and properly skilled to reduce cybersecurity risks to the enterprise.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain a Security Awareness Program
Train Workforce Members to Recognize Social Engineering Attacks
Train Workforce Members on Authentication Best Practices
Train Workforce Members on Recognizing and Reporting Security Incidents

controls.name: Service Provider Management
controls.description: Develop a process to evaluate service providers who hold sensitive data, or are responsible for an enterprise's critical IT platforms or processes, to ensure these providers are protecting those platforms and data appropriately.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Providers
Establish and Maintain a Service Provider Management Policy
Classify Service Providers
Ensure Service Provider Contracts Include Security Requirements

controls.name: Application Software Security
controls.description: Manage the security life cycle of in-house developed, hosted, or acquired software to prevent, detect, and remediate security weaknesses before they can impact the enterprise.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain a Secure Application Development Process
Establish and Maintain a Process to Accept and Address Software Vulnerabilities
Establish and Manage an Inventory of Third-Party Software Components
Train Developers in Application Security Concepts and Secure Coding

controls.name: Incident Response Management
controls.description: Establish a program to develop and maintain an incident response capability (e.g., policies, plans, procedures, defined roles, training, and communications) to prepare, detect, and quickly respond to an attack.

safeguards.name: Designate Personnel to Manage Incident Handling
Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting Security Incidents
Establish and Maintain an Enterprise Process for Reporting Incidents
Define Mechanisms for Communicating During Incident Response

controls.name: Penetration Testing
controls.description: Test the effectiveness and resiliency of enterprise assets through identifying and exploiting weaknesses in controls (people, processes, and technology), and simulating the objectives and actions of an attacker.

safeguards.name: Establish and Maintain a Penetration Testing Program
Perform Periodic External Penetration Tests
Remediate Penetration Test Findings
Validate Security Measures
